Title: Role of Ethylene Biosynthesis Genes in the Regulation of Salt Stress and Drought Stress Tolerance in Petunia

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Previous work with the same CRISPR/Cas9-edited PhACO1 lines showed reduced ethylene production, prolonged flower longevity, and impaired seed germination; this study adds that these edits also reduce salt and drought stress tolerance, highlighting a trade-off between postharvest quality and abiotic stress resilience.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhACO1 encodes an ACC oxidase involved in the final step of ethylene biosynthesis in Petunia hybrida cv. 'Mirage Rose'. 2. CRISPR/Cas9 editing of PhACO1 (phaco1 mutants) markedly reduces ACO1 transcript levels and ethylene production in leaves under both control and stress conditions. 3. phaco1 mutants are more sensitive than WT to salt (NaCl) and drought stress, showing reduced growth, lower RWC, altered stomatal behavior, and less favorable K+/Na+ homeostasis. 4. Reduced ethylene production in phaco1 mutants is associated with altered expression of antioxidant, proline-related, ABA biosynthesis/signaling, and ethylene receptor/signaling genes under stress, indicating that PhACO1-mediated ethylene production is important for transcriptional regulation of abiotic stress–responsive pathways.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss-of-function of PhACO1 via CRISPR editing lowers ethylene production in leaves and decreases tolerance to salt and drought stress, resulting in reduced growth, lower RWC, more closed stomata, and less favorable K+/Na+ ratios compared with WT plants.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: PhACO1 encodes ACC oxidase catalyzing the conversion of ACC to ethylene; CRISPR editing greatly reduces ACO1 expression and ethylene production, which in turn alters transcription of antioxidant (CAT, POD, Osmotin), ABA biosynthesis/signaling (NCED1, AAO31, PLDα), and ethylene receptor/signaling (ETR2, ERS1, EIN2, EIL1) genes during salt and drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Ethylene biosynthesis pathway and ethylene-mediated abiotic stress response, with cross-talk to ABA signaling and antioxidant/proline metabolism under salt and drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ethylene biosynthesis (ACC oxidase–catalyzed conversion of ACC to ethylene).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In WT leaves, ACO1 transcript levels are strongly induced by salt stress and by drought stress, whereas phaco1 mutants show barely detectable ACO1 expression before stress and only weak induction under drought; ACO1 is also stress-inducible in aco3 mutants. Under all conditions, ACO1 expression and ethylene production are markedly higher in WT than in phaco1 mutants.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress imposed by irrigating potted plants with 50, 100, then 150 mM NaCl, and drought stress imposed by withholding water for 10 days in a greenhouse.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Ethylene biosynthesis gene function in abiotic stress (salt and drought) tolerance in petunia.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (salt stress and drought stress).
Plant_Genes.Favorable_Allele: The wild-type PhACO1 allele, which maintains higher ethylene production, is favorable for salt and drought stress tolerance compared with CRISPR-edited phaco1 alleles.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under salt and drought stress, ethylene production in WT leaves was approximately 2–3-fold higher than in phaco1 mutants, and salt stress increased leaf Na+ content roughly 4–5-fold relative to pre-stress levels, with phaco1 mutants showing lower K+/Na+ ratios than WT.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Under salt stress, phaco1 mutants exhibited thicker leaves, slower increases in plant height and fresh weight, stronger reductions in RWC and stomatal density, and fewer open stomata than WT. Under drought stress, phaco1 mutants showed greater reductions in fresh weight and more severe leaf drop, with stomata more frequently closed than in WT.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: CRISPR/Cas9-edited PhACO1 homozygous mutants (phaco1 lines 91-1 and 36-4) and wild-type Petunia hybrida cv. 'Mirage Rose' plants grown in peat-based soil in a greenhouse.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Use of previously generated CRISPR/Cas9-edited PhACO1 mutants; greenhouse pot culture; controlled salt (NaCl) and drought treatments; measurement of plant height, leaf number, and fresh weight; assessment of RWC, stomatal density and status, chlorophyll (SPAD) values, and ion contents by ICP-OES; ethylene quantification in leaves by gas chromatography; and qRT-PCR analysis of ethylene biosynthesis, ethylene receptor/signaling, antioxidant, proline-related, and ABA biosynthesis/signaling genes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation by CRISPR/Cas9 editing of PhACO1 combined with phenotypic analysis under salt and drought stress, ethylene production assays, and qRT-PCR profiling of stress-related genes in WT and phaco1 mutants.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Reverse genetics approach using previously generated CRISPR/Cas9-edited PhACO1 lines as candidate ethylene biosynthesis mutants to test gene function in salt and drought stress tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: PhACO1 is required to maintain ethylene levels that support salt and drought stress tolerance in petunia. Reducing PhACO1 activity to prolong flower longevity can compromise abiotic stress tolerance; therefore, breeding or gene-editing strategies aiming at ethylene reduction in petunia and other horticultural crops should balance postharvest quality improvements against potential losses in salt and drought tolerance.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: CRISPR/Cas9 editing of PhACO3, previously shown to reduce ethylene and extend flower longevity, is here demonstrated to also diminish salt and drought stress tolerance, reinforcing the trade-off between ethylene-mediated ornamental traits and abiotic stress resilience.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhACO3 encodes an ACC oxidase involved in ethylene biosynthesis in Petunia hybrida cv. 'Mirage Rose'. 2. CRISPR/Cas9 editing of PhACO3 (phaco3 mutants) substantially reduces ACO3 transcript levels and ethylene production in leaves under control and stress conditions. 3. phaco3 mutants are more sensitive than WT to salt and drought stress, with reduced growth, lower RWC, altered stomatal behavior, and less favorable K+/Na+ homeostasis. 4. Reduced ethylene production in phaco3 mutants is accompanied by altered expression patterns of antioxidant, proline-related, ABA biosynthesis/signaling, and ethylene receptor/signaling genes during stress, indicating that PhACO3-mediated ethylene biosynthesis contributes to transcriptional regulation of abiotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss-of-function of PhACO3 via CRISPR editing lowers ethylene production in leaves and decreases tolerance to salt and drought stress, leading to weaker growth, lower RWC, more closed stomata, and poorer K+/Na+ homeostasis compared with WT plants.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: PhACO3 encodes ACC oxidase catalyzing the final step of ethylene biosynthesis; CRISPR disruption greatly reduces ACO3 expression and ethylene production, which modifies transcriptional responses of antioxidant, ABA biosynthesis/signaling, and ethylene signaling genes under salt and drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Ethylene biosynthesis pathway and ethylene-mediated abiotic stress response, integrated with ABA signaling and antioxidant/proline metabolism during salt and drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ethylene biosynthesis (ACC oxidase–catalyzed conversion of ACC to ethylene).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In WT leaves, ACO3 transcript levels are strongly induced by salt and drought stress, whereas phaco3 mutants show barely detectable ACO3 expression before stress and little or no significant induction under stress; ACO3 is also stress-inducible in aco1 mutants. Across conditions, ACO3 expression and ethylene production remain much higher in WT than in phaco3 mutants.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress applied by stepwise irrigation with 50, 100, then 150 mM NaCl and drought stress imposed by withholding water for 10 days in a greenhouse.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of PhACO3-mediated ethylene biosynthesis in regulating salt and drought stress tolerance in petunia.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (salt stress and drought stress).
Plant_Genes.Favorable_Allele: The wild-type PhACO3 allele, which maintains higher ethylene production, is favorable for salt and drought stress tolerance compared with CRISPR-edited phaco3 alleles.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under salt and drought stress, ethylene production in WT leaves was approximately 2–3-fold higher than in phaco3 mutants, and salt stress increased leaf Na+ content about 4–5-fold relative to pre-stress levels, with phaco3 mutants displaying lower K+/Na+ ratios than WT.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Under salt stress, phaco3 mutants showed slower increases in plant height and fresh weight, stronger declines in RWC and stomatal density, and fewer open stomata than WT, along with thicker leaf tissues. Under drought stress, phaco3 mutants experienced greater reductions in fresh weight and more extensive leaf drop, and their stomata were more often closed than those of WT plants.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: CRISPR/Cas9-edited PhACO3 homozygous mutants (phaco3 lines 14-10 and 32-15) and wild-type Petunia hybrida cv. 'Mirage Rose' plants grown in peat-based soil in a greenhouse.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Use of previously generated CRISPR/Cas9-edited PhACO3 mutants; greenhouse pot experiments; imposition of salt (NaCl) and drought stress; measurement of plant growth traits; determination of RWC, stomatal density and aperture, chlorophyll (SPAD) values, and ion contents by ICP-OES; quantification of leaf ethylene by gas chromatography; and qRT-PCR analysis of ethylene biosynthesis, ethylene receptor/signaling, antioxidant, proline-related, and ABA biosynthesis/signaling genes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation by CRISPR/Cas9 editing of PhACO3 combined with salt and drought stress phenotyping, ethylene production measurements, and qRT-PCR profiling of stress-related genes in WT and phaco3 mutants.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Reverse genetics using previously created CRISPR/Cas9-edited PhACO3 lines as candidate ethylene biosynthesis mutants to test gene function in salt and drought stress tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Functional PhACO3 is important for maintaining ethylene levels that support salt and drought stress tolerance in petunia. Strategies that reduce PhACO3 activity to prolong flower longevity may compromise plant performance under abiotic stress; breeding and gene-editing programs should therefore weigh improvements in ornamental quality against potential reductions in stress tolerance.
